title: Smoky Saudia Flaxseed Stir-fry
description: A comforting and light dish featuring smoky-flavored flaxseed in a nut-free-friendly stir-fry that combines aromatic spices to bring out the best flavors of Saudi Arabian cuisine.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: For added smokiness, consider using a few drops of liquid smoke instead

ingredients.name: 2 medium bell peppers, sliced (red and yellow mixed)
1 cup flaxseed meal
ingredients.notes: Ensure it is not pre-cooked or processed

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper (optional for extra heat)

instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and minced garlic. Sauté for about 2 minutes until softened but not browned.
Push the onions aside and add sliced bell peppers to the skillet. Stir-fry for another 3-4 minutes or until slightly softened.
Stir in the flaxseed meal, tomato paste, ground cumin, smoked paprika, and a pinch of salt (if using). Mix well with vegetables for about 1 minute, allowing the flavors to meld together.
Pour in the diced tomatoes. Stir gently so as not to break up the tomatoes too much.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let the mixture simmer for about 20-25 minutes, stirring occasionally, until most of the liquid is absorbed.
Serve hot with a side of whole-grain bread or quinoa.
